message:
feat(fcp-core): add canonical connector artifact types and ObjectId prefixed parsing

Part of the FCP3 semantic ownership migration (P3.1/P3.2): establish
fcp-core as the canonical home for durable object schemas that are
shared across registry, store, and manifest layers.

connector_artifacts.rs (new, 187 lines):
- ConnectorTarget: OS + CPU arch pairing with from_env() and as_string()
- ConnectorManifestObject: manifest TOML + hash, with SchemaId
  "fcp.core:ConnectorManifestObject@1.0.0" and signing view schema
- ConnectorBinaryObject: target + hash + binary payload, with SchemaId
  "fcp.core:ConnectorBinaryObject@1.0.0"
- ConnectorBinarySymbolSet: full symbol-layer descriptor with manifest/
  binary refs, OTI, symbol counts, timestamp, and SchemaId
  "fcp.core:ConnectorBinarySymbolSet@1.0.0"
- ConnectorBinaryTransmissionInfo: transmission parameters with
  constructor and conversion to/from store ObjectTransmissionInfo
- connector_manifest_signing_view_schema() helper for signing-view
  schema derivation

object.rs:
- ObjectId::parse_prefixed(): Parse "objectid:<hex>" or raw hex strings,
  returning ObjectIdParseError on invalid hex or wrong length
- ObjectId::to_prefixed_string(): Render as "objectid:<hex>" for
  manifest/JSON payloads
- ObjectIdParseError enum with InvalidHex and WrongLength variants

util/objectid_prefixed.rs (new, 112 lines):
- Serde helpers for "objectid:<hex>" format in human-readable serializers
- serialize/deserialize functions for ObjectId
- Nested "option" module for Option<ObjectId> fields
- Falls back to raw bytes for non-human-readable formats

lib.rs, util.rs:
- Wire new modules and re-exports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
